articles.title: Supreme Court Set to Test Trump’s Immigration Power as TPS Fight for Haitians and Syrians Reaches High Court
articles.summary: The justices are preparing to hear arguments over whether the Trump administration can end deportation protections for roughly 356,000 people, a case that could redraw the boundaries of presidential power in immigration.

articles.title: Iran Oil Shock Pushes U.S. Inflation Higher, Forcing Fed Into a Harder Balancing Act
articles.summary: March’s CPI jump, a record-low consumer sentiment reading and surging gasoline prices show how the Middle East conflict is reverberating through U.S. prices, spending and interest-rate expectations.

articles.title: As AI Enters the Scientific Method, Researchers Are Rewriting the Rules of Discovery
articles.summary: From hypothesis generation to lab automation, artificial intelligence is moving from tool to collaborator — and scientists are racing to decide what that should mean for evidence, authorship, bias, and trust.

articles.title: Tokyo’s New Museum of Narratives Wants to Turn Culture Into a Living Story
articles.summary: MoN Takanawa opened in late March with a bold premise: in an age of algorithmic feeds and shrinking attention spans, museums may need to become less like vaults and more like engines of connection.

articles.title: Ceasefire on paper, crisis in motion: Iran talks, Lebanon strikes and a region still on edge
articles.summary: A fragile two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran has slowed one front of the Middle East’s widening war, but diplomats, aid agencies and markets are still bracing for spillover as fighting continues in Lebanon and negotiators race to turn a pause into a durable settlement.

articles.title: Trump’s birthright citizenship fight becomes a test of presidential power and the 14th Amendment
articles.summary: After an extraordinary day at the Supreme Court, the justices appear skeptical of President Donald Trump’s attempt to narrow birthright citizenship — a case that could reshape immigration policy, executive authority and the meaning of constitutional guarantees.

articles.title: IMF Spring Meetings Open as War, Tariffs and Inflation Put the World Economy on Edge
articles.summary: With the IMF and World Bank gathering in Washington, policymakers are confronting a fragile mix of higher energy costs, renewed tariff pressure and stickier inflation just as growth had begun to stabilize.

articles.title: Artemis II’s 10-day lunar loop marks the first human return to the Moon since Apollo 17
articles.summary: NASA’s crewed test flight around the Moon is now over, but the scientific and strategic implications are only beginning to come into focus.

articles.title: AI’s next phase is enterprise control, not just bigger models
articles.summary: OpenAI, Microsoft, Google and Anthropic are racing to make AI agents useful inside real organizations — but the real battle in 2026 is over trust, security, and who gets to govern the machines.

articles.title: The tiny wedding economy is having a very online moment
articles.summary: A hyper-specific corner of internet culture—Discord weddings, VRChat vows, Twitch ceremonies, and digital celebrants—has grown from novelty to a real wedding niche, shaped by loneliness, fandom, and the post-pandemic remaking of ritual.
